subject: Fix warning.
author: Sorgelig
provenance_doc_ref: docs/patch-provenance.md row: e82a59280, 8908e0fe1, ed8f8e6ce | Fanatec wheels (+build/warning fixes)

impact.effect_if_absent: Compiler warning about unused variables (usbhid_device and current_period in ftecff_timer function)

notes: GROUPED ENTRY in provenance.md: This commit is listed with e82a59280 and 8908e0fe1 as a single row in docs/patch-provenance.md rather than individually. The commit comments out two unused variables (usbhid_device and current_period in ftecff_timer) to fix compiler warnings. Patch header (0012-hid-fanatec.patch) states this was 'folded in' during the 5.15→6.18.38 forward-port. However, the actual patch content does not show these variables commented out; instead, they are simply absent from the forward-ported code. The patch was created from scratch for the 6.18 version (files are marked as new in the patch), and the developer (Michael C. Ferguson) did a clean forward-port that removed unused code. The effect of the warning fix (eliminating unused variable warnings) is achieved, but through re-implementation rather than cherry-picking the exact commit. The patch header also mentions dropping other unused variables (s1/s2 in ftecff_update_slot and ret in ftec_tuning_write) as part of the forward-port's -Wunused-but-set-variable cleanup.
